prompt:
I've been logging lifts in a notes app for two years and I'm done. I want a little CLI, entry point workout.py, everything in one JSON file passed as --log FILE, stdlib only. I'll wire it to shell aliases so exit codes and exact output matter — treat this as the spec.

`add DATE EXERCISE --set REPSxWEIGHT [--set ...]` records one training session, silent on success. Multiple adds for the same date+exercise pile into the same session. Exercise names are case-insensitive (Bench == bench) and everything prints lowercase. Reps: positive integer; weight: positive number, decimals fine (5x102.5). Sloppy dates (2026-3-02), impossible dates (2026-02-30), malformed sets, or no --set at all: complain on stderr, exit 2, and record NOTHING. Weights and volumes always print without trailing zeros — 105, not 105.0; 102.5 stays 102.5.

`pr EXERCISE` prints exactly two lines and exits 0:
best weight: 105 kg x 3 (2026-03-06)
best e1rm: 119.6 kg (2026-03-04)
Best weight is the heaviest set ever; ties broken by more reps, then by the earlier date. e1rm is Epley — weight * (1 + reps/30) — rounded to one decimal, best across every set ever, earlier date wins ties. Exercise never trained: print nothing, exit 1.

`suggest EXERCISE` is my progression brain, one line, rules applied in THIS order (a session's 'top' = its heaviest weight):
1. plateau: three or more sessions and the top hasn't moved across the last three -> 'EXERCISE: deload to X kg' where X is 90% of the top, rounded DOWN to the nearest 2.5.
2. every set in the most recent session made 5+ reps -> 'EXERCISE: increase to X kg', top + 2.5.
3. otherwise -> 'EXERCISE: hold at W kg'.
Two equal tops in a row isn't a plateau yet, that's just Tuesday. No sessions: exit 1, silent.

`report YYYY-Www` (ISO week, like 2026-W10, two-digit week) sums the week's volume per exercise — volume = sum of reps*weight over every set whose date falls in that ISO week — one line per exercise, alphabetical: 'squat: 1315 kg (3 sets)' — and it's '(1 set)' singular when it's one. Nothing trained that week: print nothing, exit 1. A week that isn't YYYY-Www or doesn't exist: stderr, exit 2.

pr/suggest/report on a --log that doesn't exist behave like an empty log (and must NOT create the file); a log that exists but isn't valid JSON is stderr + exit 2 everywhere.

test_workout.py is the acceptance contract, run with python3 test_workout.py, hands off. Don't dump everything in one file — I want the storage layer, the stats/PR math, and the progression rules as importable modules with workout.py as the CLI on top.
